post_content: Frontier Bowmen, founded in 1961, is a well-established archery club in Regina offering both indoor and outdoor facilities. The indoor range at 2255 14th Avenue (basement) features 8 lanes at 18 metres with 24/7 member access. The outdoor range at Botkin's Pits north of the city features field and 3D courses. Members of all ages and abilities are welcome, from bow hunting to competitive target shooting.
business_hours: 24/7 member access (indoor restricted Tue/Wed 6-9PM during JOP season)

post_content: Wascana Archers is a nonprofit, 100% volunteer-operated archery club in the Regina area. They operate an indoor range at 109 Hodsman Road and a 60-acre outdoor range approximately 25 km north of Regina with 24/7 member access, trails, and open fields.

The Saskatoon Straight Shooters Archery Club is a family-friendly, inclusive nonprofit club affiliated with the SAA. They use the Saskatoon Wildlife Federation's 7000+ sq ft indoor range from October to April and an outdoor range at SWF from May onwards. Club night is Tuesdays.

Bowbusters Archery Club in Vanscoy offers indoor and outdoor archery ranges with 24/7 access for members. Annual club membership is $175 plus a $50 SAA membership. Non-members may shoot as guests up to two times under SAA insurance rules.

Battle River Archers is an active archery club based in Battleford, Saskatchewan. Adult members enjoy range access Monday and Thursday 7-9PM and Tuesday 6-9PM. JOP and Intro to Archery programs run on Tuesdays 6-7:30 PM and Wednesdays 7-9 PM, during which the range is reserved for youth programming.

Thunderhead Archers, established in 1988, operates both an indoor target range (November to April) and an outdoor range (May to October) in Delisle, Saskatchewan. The club welcomes archers of all skill levels and is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.

Thunder Creek Archery Club is a community archery club located in Moose Jaw, Saskatchewan. They host indoor shooting sessions and are affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association. The club welcomes archers of all skill levels.

post_address: Central Avenue North (Prince Albert Wildlife Federation)
post_content: Timberland Bow Benders is an archery club based in Prince Albert, Saskatchewan, operating out of the Prince Albert Wildlife Federation grounds on Central Avenue North. The club regularly hosts major provincial competitions and welcomed nearly 800 archers at their 2025 provincial shoot — the largest archery shoot in Canada.

Estevan Archery Club offers both indoor and outdoor archery facilities south of Estevan on Highway 47. The outdoor range extends west on Highway 18 towards the boundary dams. The club is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association and meets on the 1st Wednesday of every month at 7:30PM.

Swift Current Archery Club holds shooting sessions at the Lieutenant Colonel Clifton Centre in Swift Current, Saskatchewan. The facility is a former armory containing a gymnasium and range. The club is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association and welcomes archers of all skill levels.

post_address: Hwy 303 East, 2km N on Range Road 3265
post_content: Lloydminster & Area Archers operates a versatile facility 15 km east of Lloydminster at the Lloydminster Fish & Game Youth Center. The heated well-lit indoor range accommodates up to 18 archers at distances up to 40 yards with nine 4x4 repositionable target butts. Outdoor facilities include a FITA range (10-50m) and a Field & 3D course. The Youth Center is closed mid-June to mid-August.

No.1 Archery & Sports is a family-owned commercial indoor archery range and pro shop established in 1966, located approximately 10 km east of Regina on Highway 1 East in Sherwood No. 159. They offer retail archery and hunting supplies alongside an automated indoor shooting range.

Antler River Archery Club is a community archery club based in Carievale, Saskatchewan. The club operates a 5-lane indoor range and is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association. Contact Gord Mann or Ken Martel for details.

Apple Shot Archers Inc is an archery club established in 2023 in Macoun, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association. The club shoots out of the Macoun indoor gun club and is led by founder and head coach Nicole Grass. The club offers coaching and assistance for archers of all skill levels.
Assiniboine River Archery Club is a community archery club based in Canora, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.

post_title: Bad to the Bow Archery Club
post_content: Bad to the Bow Archery Club is a community archery club based in Leoville, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Beaver River Archery Club is a community archery club in Meadow Lake, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.

post_address: Yellowhead Highway West (19km west of Yorkton)
post_content: Broken Arrow Archery Club, established in 1969, is located approximately 19 km west of Yorkton on the Yellowhead Highway. The club features an outdoor 3D course with animal and bag targets and a small campground with firepit and picnic tables. The club meets Wednesdays at 6 PM. Annual adult membership is $25 including family camping access.
Bruno Bowmen is a community archery club based in Bruno, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Cypress Hills Archery Club is a community archery club based in Maple Creek, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Fleming Archery is a community archery club based in the Moosomin area of Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.

Langenburg & District Archery Club is a nonprofit volunteer-operated club established in 2018 in Langenburg, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association. The club offers both indoor and outdoor ranges for members.
Moose Creek Archers is a community archery club based at the Memorial Hall in Oxbow, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Prairie to Pines Archery Club is a community archery club in the Sturgis area of Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
River Valley Archery Club is a community archery club based in Veregin, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Spiritwood Spirited Arrows is a community archery club based in Spiritwood, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Tru Flite Archery is a community archery club based in Melville
post_tags: Saskatchewan. Contact Pam Schmuck at the number listed. The club is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.

post_content: Valley Arrowheads Archery Club is a community archery club based in Osler, Saskatchewan, affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
Viscount Archery Club operates out of a converted curling rink in Viscount, Saskatchewan. The club offers a Junior Olympic Program and is affiliated with the Saskatchewan Archery Association.
